name: vtm_v5_merits_flaws
description: V:tM V5 Merits and Flaws reference: Physical, Mental, Social, Supernatural, Thin-blood, Ghoul, Haven, Mortal Connections, Feeding, plus the Loresheet framework. Pairs with vtm_v5_core_mechanics.json and vtm_v5_disciplines_detailed.json.

0.content:
MERITS AND FLAWS — ALWAYS LOADED REFERENCE

Merits and Flaws define a kindred's edge cases beyond Attributes, Skills, and Disciplines: physical quirks, social standing, haven quality, retainers, mortal connections, supernatural taint, and esoteric backgrounds.

Mechanically:
- Merits cost dots at character creation (typically 7 dots split across categories) or experience after creation (rating × 3).
- Flaws grant dots back at creation (up to 2 dots in Flaws may be exchanged for additional Merit dots) and cannot be bought off without narrative resolution.
- Each Merit/Flaw lists rating (•, ••, •••, ••••, •••••), category, and game effect.
- Some Merits and Flaws are paired (Beautiful/Ugly, Wealthy/Destitute) — a character cannot hold both poles of a pair.

Categories covered in this lorebook:
- Physical (entry 1): body, appearance, vampiric vitality
- Mental (entry 2): cognition, memory, derangement
- Social (entry 3): standing, fame, allies, herd
- Haven (entry 4): the lair itself
- Supernatural (entry 5): blood-tied edges and curses
- Feeding and Bane (entry 6): vessel access, predation quirks
- Mortal Connections (entry 7): contacts, retainers, Touchstones-as-resource
- Thin-Blood specific (entry 8): Duskborn-only options
- Ghoul Merits (entry 9): for vitae-bound mortal allies
- Coterie Merits (entry 10): shared by a player group
- Linguistics, Resources, Status (entry 11): cross-cutting practical Merits
- Loresheets (entries 12-13): kit-specific narrative packages
- Builder protocol (entry 14): how the AI applies Merits/Flaws in play

When a player invokes a Merit during play, locate it below, name the mechanical effect, and apply. When a Flaw triggers (failed roll, scene transition, in-fiction provocation), call the consequence explicitly so the player can react.

1.content:
PHYSICAL MERITS

- Beautiful (••): striking appearance. +1 die on Social rolls where looks matter. Toreador and Ventrue gravitate to this Merit.
- Stunning (••••): supernaturally beautiful. +2 dice on Social rolls where looks matter. Mortals stop and stare; conspicuous in Masquerade-strained scenes.
- Tiny (•): small frame (under 1.55m). +1 die on Stealth rolls; -1 Health box. Useful for infiltrators, problematic in physical confrontations.
- Huge (••••): massive build (over 2m, heavy). +1 Health box; -1 die on Stealth and Drive rolls in standard vehicles.
- Iron Gullet (•••): can digest blood that would normally fail to slake — bagged, animal at high BP, partially-coagulated, even thin-blood degraded vitae. Required for Bagger and Graverobber predator types at higher Blood Potency.
- Eat Food (••): can consume mortal food and drink without immediate revulsion or vomiting. Cannot derive nourishment but provides Masquerade cover. Standard Thin-blood ability extended occasionally to Embraced kindred.
- Unflappable (•): immune to Daunt and minor Dread Gaze effects (level 1 Presence). Ignored by lower-rating Animalism.
- Bloodhound (•): identify resonance and recent feeding history of any kindred or vessel by smell alone, no roll required.

PHYSICAL FLAWS

- Ugly (•): unattractive. -1 die on Social rolls where looks matter.
- Repulsive (••): physically disturbing. -2 dice on Social rolls where looks matter; mortal NPCs require Composure rolls to remain in conversation. (Standard for Nosferatu — covered by clan Bane.)
- Stench (•): the user emits a faint corpse-odour even with hygiene. -1 die on Stealth in close quarters; mortals find them off-putting.
- Disfigured (••): visible mutilation (scarring, missing features). -2 dice on Social rolls outside the Nosferatu/criminal underworld; +1 die on Intimidation.
- Deformity (••): physical impairment (limp, malformed limb, lisp). -1 die on a specific category of Physical or Social rolls (chosen at character creation).
- Infectious Bite (••): victims of the user's bite contract a wasting illness over weeks. Stains on every feeding from a vessel intended to survive.
- Organovore (••): can only feed by consuming flesh and organ matter, not by drinking. Severe Masquerade strain at every feeding.
- Methuselah's Thirst (•••): the user can only slake Hunger by drinking the vitae of older kindred (lower generation than self). Crippling for younger kindred; routine for elders.

KIT NOTES:
- Bishop Callista Vox's Brisbane retinue includes pre-Wallachian Tzimisce subjects whose Fleshcrafting has imposed Stunning or Disfigured at the Bishop's aesthetic discretion.
- Nosferatu in Sarrasine's Sydney work the Repulsive/Stench combination as institutional camouflage; Sarrasine's methuselah-tempo register tolerates them precisely because they are reliably overlooked.

2.content:
MENTAL MERITS

- Common Sense (•): once per session, the player may ask the narrator whether a planned action is likely to fail catastrophically; the narrator answers honestly within in-fiction limits.
- Calm Heart (••): +2 dice to resist Fury Frenzy. Stacks with Composure-based pools.
- Unbondable (•••••): cannot form a Blood Bond as the bonded party. Vampires and Tremere alike consider this Merit a dangerous rumour and a dangerous fact.
- Eat Food: see Physical Merits.
- Eidetic Memory (••): perfectly recall any scene the user has experienced; +2 dice on rolls to recognise faces, recall conversations, retrace movements.
- Time Sense (•): always know the current time within five minutes; useful for sunrise calculations and surveillance work.
- Untouchable (•••): once per session, ignore the consequences of a single Bestial Failure (the roll still fails, but no Compulsion or Stain triggers).
- Light Sleeper (•): wake at the first sound or movement during daysleep, though slow to act for the first turn.
- Code of Honour (••): the user's Convictions count as twice their normal weight for Willpower recovery; Stains for violating the Code are doubled.

MENTAL FLAWS

- Bad Memory (••): -2 dice on rolls to recall details, recognise faces, or retrace movements.
- Beacon of Profanity (••): the user emits an aura that nearby True Faith-touched mortals (clergy, sincere believers) sense within metres. Auspex users on alert detect the user automatically.
- Prey Exclusion (••): cannot feed from a specific category of mortal (children, students, addicts, working dock crews — chosen at character creation). Stains and frenzy resistance penalty if forced.
- Stake Bait (••): -2 dice to resist staking attempts; the user's heart sits a fraction of an inch off-centre and stakes find it more readily.
- Phobia (•-•••): irrational fear of a specific stimulus (fire — already innate; small spaces; mirrors; mortal children; specific named entity). Triggers Terror Frenzy resistance at +1 to +3 Difficulty depending on rating.
- Compulsive Liar (••): cannot tell direct truth in social situations without spending Willpower; -2 dice on Persuasion rolls where sincerity matters.
- Forgetful (•): each session, lose a single piece of information the player would otherwise have remembered (narrator chooses or rolls).
- Obsessed (••): fixate on a topic, person, or goal that compels at least one significant action per session toward it. Failing to act inflicts 1 Superficial Willpower damage.
- Amnesia (••): cannot recall significant events from before the Embrace; the past is a usable narrative resource for the narrator at any time.
- Vengeful (•-••): cannot rest without acting on a perceived wrong. Each session, must take an action against the wrongdoer or suffer 1 Superficial Willpower.
- Hopeless Addict (••): bound to a mortal substance (heroin, amphetamines, alcohol, prescription opioids) that vitae partially carries. Withdrawal inflicts -2 dice penalties; the user feeds preferentially on vessels carrying the substance.

KIT NOTES:
- Lytton's Beckoning trajectory begins to manifest as occasional intrusive thoughts of pre-Embrace England — a narrative-only effect, not codified as Amnesia, but Storytellers may treat it functionally as a partial Bad Memory at advanced trajectory.
- Cardinal Aurelius's acute Beckoning manifests as Obsession (the call) at chronicle-active intensity.

3.content:
SOCIAL MERITS

- Allies (•-•••): mortal organisations or significant individuals who provide aid. Rating reflects scope: 1 dot = a small business owner or local journalist; 5 dots = a federal agency contact, a Mossman-tier law firm, a city-newspaper editor. Allies require maintenance — favours, money, attention. Each session of neglect risks reduction.
- Contacts (•-•••): mortals who provide specific information on request. 1 dot = one specific person who knows one specific subject; 5 dots = a network covering an entire field (Brisbane Valley nightlife, Melbourne federal politics, Sydney harbour logistics).
- Mawla (•-•••): a mentor among the kindred who provides guidance, occasional intervention, and political cover. 1 dot = an ancilla who returns calls; 5 dots = an elder Prince or methuselah ally. Lytton has served as Mawla to several Camarilla orthodox Melbourne neonates; Octavia maintains Mawla relationships with Adelaide Hills surrounds.
- Herd (•-•••): a stable group of willing or conditioned mortal vessels. 1 dot = a few regular vessels; 5 dots = a cult, an entire club's regulars, a Lingering Kiss-saturated circle. Reduces feeding rolls; sized to chronicle.
- Status (•-•••••): standing in a kindred polity. 1 dot = recognised neonate; 3 dots = harpy or sheriff equivalent; 5 dots = Prince, Cardinal, Sect-defining figure. Each domain runs its own Status track. Lytton, Sarrasine, Bishop Callista, Octavia, and the three refusenik Cardinals are 5-dot figures in their respective registers.
- Fame (•-•••••): mortal renown. 1 dot = local celebrity; 3 dots = nationally recognised; 5 dots = global icon. Adds dice to Social rolls with mortals who recognise the user; severe Masquerade strain at every public appearance.
- Resources (•-•••): personal wealth. 1 dot = comfortable; 3 dots = quite wealthy; 5 dots = methuselah-fortune (off-shore portfolios, real-estate empires, generational holdings).
- True Love (••): a single named mortal whose love anchors the user. +2 dice to resist Compulsions and Frenzy when their wellbeing is at stake. Often paired with Touchstone status.
- Pack Defender (•-••): the user is recognised by a coterie or pack as their primary protector; gain dice when defending coterie-mates.
- Mask (•-••): a constructed false identity with paperwork, residence, and digital footprint. 1 dot = passes routine checks; 2 dots = passes deep background investigation. Useful for kindred operating across decades.
- Zeroed (••): no records of the user exist in mortal databases — government, medical, financial. Achieved through specialist labour over years. Allies in IT and bureaucracy maintain the void.
- Cobbler (•): can produce or commission a Mask within days rather than months.

SOCIAL FLAWS

- Adversary / Enemy (•-•••): a named individual who actively opposes the user. 1 dot = nuisance with limited reach; 3 dots = peer-tier kindred or organised mortal group; 5 dots = elder, Prince, Sabbat pack, federal taskforce.
- Shunned (••): a specific kindred polity (one Camarilla domain, one Anarch barony, one Sabbat pack) refuses to recognise the user; cannot claim hospitality.
- Infamy (••): mortal reputation for something specific and bad. -2 dice on Social rolls with mortals who recognise the user.
- Suspect (•): a major kindred faction (Camarilla, Anarch, Sabbat, Hecata) regards the user with default suspicion.
- Despised (•••): an entire kindred faction wishes the user dead. Active hunt at chronicle pace.
- Dark Secret (•-••): a fact about the user that, if revealed, would destroy their standing or trigger lethal response. 1 dot = embarrassing; 2 dots = catastrophic (diablerie record, broken Masquerade event, working with hunters).
- Disliked (•): a specific community (a coterie, a court, a neighbourhood) finds the user grating; -1 die on Social rolls with members.
- Stalkers (•-•): mortals fixate obsessively on the user; constant Masquerade pressure and feeding-pattern leakage.

KIT NOTES:
- Templar Constance Abernathy holds Status ••• in Brisbane Sabbat-survivor structures and a 50-year Mawla-equivalent correspondence with Cardinal Reyna.
- The Footscray Crew (Anarch coterie) operates at Status •• in Anarch movement and Status • Shunned by Lytton's orthodox court.
- Pentex First Team operatives are typical Adversary ••• threats for cross-supernatural diplomatic chronicles.

4.content:
HAVEN — the kindred's daysleep refuge. A haven Merit-package describes its quality, defenses, location, and resources.

HAVEN BASE (••): standard daysleep-secure residence with light-tight rooms and basic security. Most kindred protagonists own at least this rating.

HAVEN MERITS (added on top of base, dot-by-dot):

- Hidden Armoury (•): a concealed weapons cache (firearms, blades, stakes-for-others, fire weapons). Acceptably stocked for the chronicle's expected combat tier.
- Cell (•): a soundproof, lockable holding room for vessels, prisoners, or interrogation. Standard equipment for Tithe and Cleaver predator types.
- Watchmen (•-••): mortal guards (1 dot = night watchman; 2 dots = small staff with credible response capacity).
- Laboratory (•-•••): equipment for Blood Sorcery rituals, Thin-Blood Alchemy Fixatio Method, mortal-grade forensics, or Tzimisce flesh-craft preparation.
- Library (•-•••): occult, historical, or specialised reference. 1 dot = a few useful volumes; 3 dots = research-grade collection. Adds dice to Intelligence + Academics or Occult rolls performed at the haven.
- Luxury (•): expensive furnishings and trappings. +1 die on Social rolls conducted at the haven where impressiveness matters.
- Postern (•): a hidden secondary exit (sewer access, neighbouring building, tunnel). +2 dice on Stealth or escape rolls departing the haven.
- Security System (•-•••): electronic surveillance, alarms, biometric locks. Each dot adds 2 dice to detection of intruders.
- Surgery (•): equipped for trauma-grade medical work (and feeding-related cleanup).
- Warding (••-•••): Blood Sorcery or Oblivion-ceremony defences against supernatural intrusion. Requires the appropriate Discipline-trained kindred to maintain.
- Location (•-••): situational advantage. 1 dot = strategically placed near key target territory; 2 dots = defensible terrain (hilltop, isolated peninsula, secured harbour edge).
- Holdings (•-•••): the haven generates passive income through legitimate or front businesses; can substitute for low Resources at narrator discretion.

HAVEN FLAWS:

- No Haven (••): no fixed daysleep refuge. Every day requires improvised sheltering; sunrise risk every dawn.
- Compromised (••): a kindred enemy, hunter cell, or mortal authority knows the haven's location.
- Creepy (•): the haven exudes wrongness; mortal staff and guests refuse to stay long; -1 die on Social rolls held there.
- Haunted (••): a wraith or other restless dead inhabits the haven; intermittent Stains and disruptions at narrator discretion.
- Strict Curfew (•): the building has external rules (apartment building hours, gated community, federal facility) that constrain coming and going.
- Shared (•): the haven is shared with another kindred or mortal household; privacy and security compromised.

KIT NOTES:
- Lytton's Melbourne haven: Luxury ••, Library •••, Security System •••, Watchmen ••, Holdings ••• (ground-floor mortal-fronted gallery in inner-east).
- Sarrasine's Sydney haven: Luxury •, Library •••••, Postern ••, Warding •••, Location •• (harbour-edge, sub-basement levels of an old warehouse repurposed across decades).
- Bishop Callista Vox's Brisbane haven: Laboratory ••• (flesh-craft), Cell, Watchmen, Surgery (Brisbane Sabbat-survivor signature configuration).
- Octavia's Adelaide haven: Luxury, Library ••, Holdings (Adelaide Hills small-domain wine estate front).
- Templar Constance Abernathy's haven: Postern ••, Hidden Armoury, Compromised •• (the 50-year correspondence channel is a known asset to enemies).
- The Footscray Crew shared haven: Shared •, Holdings • (a working garage), Postern, Hidden Armoury — Anarch-baseline configuration.

5.content:
SUPERNATURAL MERITS — edge cases of vampiric blood and condition beyond standard Disciplines.

- Bloodhound: see Physical (vessel-resonance scent).
- Iron Gullet: see Physical.
- Eat Food: see Physical.
- Pierce Steel's Curtain (••): can perceive directly into the wraith-realm without Auspex or Oblivion (typically Hecata-adjacent). Triggers visions; Storyteller may impose Stains for prolonged exposure.
- Reclaimed Humanity (••): the user has rebuilt Humanity from a low point; gain +2 dice on Remorse rolls.
- True Faith (•••••): the user (extremely rare for kindred — usually ex-clergy with profound personal conviction surviving Embrace) projects holy aura. Inflicts dread on uncomfortable kindred and burns at the touch of unholy ones; kindred carriers themselves suffer ongoing Stains and frenzy pressure. Almost always an antagonist trait, occasionally a tragic protagonist signature.
- Soul of the Slain (••): once per chronicle, communicate with a mortal the user fed to death. Single conversation; severe Stains.
- Knowledgeable Coterie (•): the coterie counts as one rating higher in a single shared Skill specialty (chosen at creation).
- Unbondable: see Mental Merits.

SUPERNATURAL FLAWS

- Diablerist's Blood (••): the user is a known or detectable diablerist. Auspex 3 (Scry the Soul) reveals black-veined aura; standing Stains at the start of every session.
- Soul Stained (•): the user's aura reads as wrong to all Auspex 3+ users — the cause may be a single past act, the proximity of a methuselah's attention, or a Beckoning-touch.
- Haunted (••): a specific wraith or shadow follows the user. Periodic disruption; the wraith may have its own agenda.
- Bond Junkie (•): once Bonded, the user resists breaking the bond at increased difficulty (-2 dice on bond-resistance rolls).
- Bond Resistant (•): inverse, treated as a Merit or Flaw depending on chronicle (a Sabbat-survivor sees it as Merit; a Camarilla orthodox court treats it as Flaw because bonded loyalty is institutional currency).
- Unstable Aura (••): aura colours and Auspex readings shift unpredictably; -2 dice on Subterfuge rolls against Auspex users.
- Beckoned (•-•••): the call from the East tugs at the user. Each rating adds a dice penalty to resist Compulsions to travel toward the Middle East / methuselah convergence points. Lytton at intermediate trajectory operates at Beckoned •; Cardinal Reyna also intermediate; Cardinal Aurelius acute is Beckoned •••.
- Stigmata (••): the user bleeds spontaneously from palms, eyes, or forehead at moments of stress or holy proximity. Masquerade strain at every public moment.
- Touched by the Beast (•): one randomly determined Compulsion (per Storyteller pick) cannot be refused with Willpower.

KIT NOTES:
- Cardinal Aurelius: Beckoned ••• (acute trajectory) plus Soul Stained — chronicle-active condition feeding into 2025-2027 chronicle calendar.
- Lytton: Beckoned • (intermediate trajectory).
- Cardinal Reyna: Beckoned • (intermediate trajectory).
- Sarrasine: Beckoned 0 — methuselah-tempo Sydney has so far insulated against the call, narrator-flagged as fragile.
- Bishop Callista Vox: Soul Stained (the pre-Wallachian flesh-craft register registers as wrong to standard Auspex readings even when the Bishop has done nothing recently culpable).

6.comment: ENTRY 6 — Feeding and Bane Merits and Flaws
6.content:
FEEDING-RELATED MERITS

- Iron Gullet: see Physical (digest degraded blood).
- Bloodhound: see Physical (resonance scent).
- High-Functioning (••): once per scene, suppress visible Hunger 4 effects (red eyes, fang prominence, twitching). Useful for Camarilla orthodox public engagements at Lytton's or Octavia's courts.
- Mythic Resonance (••): a specific vessel type the user has identified produces Resonance Dyscrasia (temporary discipline-flavoured boon) reliably for them. Storyteller defines.
- Bloodbond Veteran (••): the user has experienced and broken at least one Blood Bond and recognises the early stages in others. +2 dice on Wits + Insight to detect bonds.
- Farmer (••): can sustain on animal blood at higher Blood Potency than standard rules allow (effectively raises animal-blood viability cap by 1 BP).

FEEDING-RELATED FLAWS

- Prey Exclusion (••): cannot feed on a specific category (children, students, addicts, pregnant women, working dock crews, kindred). Stains and frenzy resistance penalty if forced.
- Farmer (•) — Flaw form: cannot bring self to feed on humans; restricted to animal blood. (Note: Predator type Farmer overlaps; choose one framing.)
- Vegan (•): inverse-Farmer: cannot feed from animals at all; restricted to mortal blood. Common for Toreador with Aesthetic Fixation.
- Methuselah's Thirst: see Physical (only feeds on lower-generation kindred).
- Organovore: see Physical (must eat flesh, not drink).
- Prey Fidelity (•): forms attachment to repeat vessels; loses 1 Willpower per session if a fed-upon vessel is harmed by another.
- Bestial Temper (••): resist Hunger Frenzy at +1 Difficulty.
- Folkloric Bane (•): a specific traditional anti-vampire substance (silver, iron, salt, garlic) inflicts an extra die of Aggravated when used against the user.
- Folkloric Block (•): cannot enter a place protected by a specific traditional ward (running water, threshold without invitation, consecrated ground).
- Stake Bait: see Mental.
- Starving Decay (•): if Hunger reaches 5, the user's flesh visibly decays; severe Masquerade strain until fed.

BANE SEVERITY (clan-rooted)

A character's clan Bane scales with Blood Potency (capped per clan). Some Banes have Merits or Flaws that interact:

- Soothed Beast (• Merit, Brujah only): -1 to Bane Severity for Violent Temper resistance rolls.
- Aristocratic Tastes (varies, Ventrue): the rarefied vessel restriction can be re-rolled at character creation if a chronicle-relevant choice is made (Lytton's vessels are dock-worker mortals with specific tattoos — a chronicle-rooted Ventrue feeding restriction; Octavia's vessels are Adelaide Hills small-business owners with specific accent markers).
- Faithful Servant (• Merit, Lasombra/Hecata): an existing wraith or shadow companion accompanies the user without ceremony or upkeep.
- Guarded Fetter (• Merit, Hecata): the wraith bound to the user is harder to forcibly unbind.
- Lingering Soil (• Flaw, Tzimisce): the soil-of-significance the user must rest in is increasingly difficult to source — the original location has been disturbed, redeveloped, or destroyed. Bishop Callista Vox carries a chronicle-relevant version: the Wallachian soil-source predates redrawn borders.

KIT NOTES:
- Lytton's Ventrue feeding restriction (Aristocratic Tastes): dock-worker mortals with specific tattoos — a Melbourne-historical pattern reaching back to the late Victorian harbour. Hunting requires presence at specific docks; a major chronicle hook.
- Octavia's Ventrue feeding restriction: Adelaide Hills small-business owners with specific local-cultural markers; rural feeding requires drives out of metro Adelaide.
- Sarrasine's Toreador Aesthetic Fixation operates at methuselah-tempo intensity; entire scenes can be derailed by a single beautiful object.
- Cardinal Magdalena's Tzimisce Grounded condition draws on Russian Far East soil specifically; chronicle complications occur if the Cardinal must travel beyond the territory.

7.comment: ENTRY 7 — Mortal Connections (Allies, Contacts, Retainers, Touchstones)
7.content:
MORTAL CONNECTIONS — the protagonist's living network. Distinct from Touchstones (which serve a Humanity function) but often overlapping.

ALLIES (•-•••): mortal organisations or significant individuals providing aid. Each session, may call on Allies for one favour scaled to rating; significant favours may require contested rolls or in-fiction effort. Allies have agendas; over-use risks rebellion or exposure.

CONTACTS (•-•••): mortal informants. Each contact answers questions or makes introductions in a defined area. Contacts do not generally take risk for the user; they trade information.

RETAINERS (•-•••): mortals (sometimes ghouls) personally bound to the user, taking direct orders and accepting risk. 1 dot = a single trusted assistant (driver, secretary, security); 3 dots = a small staff. Retainers may be ghouls (see Ghoul Merits entry) or unbonded.

HERD (•-•••): see Social (stable feeding pool).

TRUE LOVE (••): see Social (named mortal anchoring).

TOUCHSTONE-AS-RESOURCE: a Touchstone is mechanically a Conviction-anchor for Humanity, not a Merit per se. However, narrative interactions with Touchstones can substitute for low-rating Allies or Contacts at narrator discretion — the Brisbane mortal courier who has handled drop-letters for 50 years between Templar Constance and Cardinal Reyna is simultaneously the Templar's Touchstone and a 1-dot Ally for the Cardinal correspondence channel.

MORTAL CONNECTION FLAWS

- Dependent (•): a specific mortal relies on the user for protection, money, or access to drugs/medicine. Each session, must spend at least one scene's worth of attention on the dependent or take 1 Superficial Willpower.
- Hunted (•••): a specific mortal organisation (federal agency, hunter cell, organised criminal group) actively pursues the user.
- Enemy: see Social.
- Stalkers: see Social.
- Disliked: see Social.

KIT NOTES:
- Lytton (Melbourne) maintains Allies ••• (legal firm specialising in inner-east property), Contacts •• (federal Liberal-aligned political office staff), Retainers ••• (3 ghouls, including a 60-year tenured estate manager), Herd ••.
- Sarrasine (Sydney) maintains Allies ••••• (cross-Pacific shipping company chairwoman, third-generation), Contacts ••••, Retainers •• (2 long-tenured ghouls), Herd •.
- Bishop Callista Vox (Brisbane) maintains Retainers ••• (Brisbane Sabbat-survivor cell members, mixed ghoul/sworn-mortal), Allies • (Brisbane Valley nightlife operators), Contacts •.
- Octavia (Adelaide) maintains Allies ••, Contacts •• (Adelaide Hills small-business owners, partly her feeding pool), Retainers ••, Herd •.
- Templar Constance Abernathy maintains Retainers • (the 50-year mortal courier), Allies • (legal-firm contact handling Brisbane Sabbat-survivor cover identities).
- The Footscray Crew shared coterie maintains Allies • (working dock crew association), Contacts •• (Footscray underground music scene), Herd ••.

Mortal connections require maintenance. A session-by-session log of ally/contact/retainer interaction prevents drift; sustained neglect reduces ratings.

8.comment: ENTRY 8 — Thin-Blood Specific Merits and Flaws
8.content:
THIN-BLOOD MERITS (Duskborn — 14th-16th generation only)

Thin-bloods select Thin-Blood Merits and Flaws as a distinct package at character creation; some Merits/Flaws are mandatory (typically 1 Merit + 1 Flaw).

MERITS

- Catenating Blood (•): can create Blood Bonds and ghouls normally — atypical for Thin-bloods who usually cannot.
- Day Drinker (•): can endure direct sunlight without immediate Aggravated damage — sunlight inflicts Superficial only, accumulating across hours rather than turns. The defining Duskborn signature.
- Discipline Affinity (•): selects one non-Alchemy Discipline as in-clan-equivalent; learns at standard rather than Caitiff cost.
- Lifelike (•): heart beats faintly, body warms slightly, can blush. Passes routine medical examination; defeats Auspex 1.
- Thin-Blood Alchemist (•): can learn and prepare Alchemy formulae (most Thin-bloods need this Merit to access the discipline; not all have it).
- Vampiric Tongue (•): can lick wounds closed and grant Lingering Kiss-equivalent on a vessel.
- Anarch Comrades (•): the Anarch Movement accepts the user without prejudice; full Status • among Anarchs.
- Low Humanity (•): begins play with Humanity 5 instead of 7; in exchange, +2 dice on Frenzy resistance and starts with one more Discipline dot.

FLAWS

- Baby Teeth (•): fangs are stunted and unreliable; -2 dice on bite-feeding rolls; Brutal Feed and similar powers unavailable.
- Bestial Temper: see Feeding.
- Branded by the Camarilla (•): a specific Camarilla domain (often the local one) has registered the user as a tolerated nuisance; cannot rise above Status 0 in that polity. In Lytton's Melbourne, all Thin-bloods are Branded by default.
- Clan Curse (•): suffers a randomly determined clan Bane (rolled at character creation) at Bane Severity 1, despite having no clan.
- Dead Flesh (•): does not pass for living — body cold, no pulse, visible corpse signs. Inverse of Lifelike.
- Mortal Frailty (•): cannot Mend Superficial damage with a Rouse Check; heals at mortal speeds.
- Vitae Dependency (•): must drink kindred vitae weekly or suffer accumulating Hunger that cannot be slaked from mortal blood.
- Heliophobia (•): suffers Terror Frenzy resistance at +1 Difficulty against any sunlight even when not damaging (defeats Day Drinker socially).
- Plague Bearer (•): the user's blood carries a transmissible vampiric infection — vessels may turn into Thin-bloods themselves over weeks. Stains accumulate easily.
- Shunned by the Anarchs (•): the Anarch Movement refuses recognition; cannot claim hospitality or Status.

KIT NOTES:
- Day Drinker Thin-bloods are the only kindred with realistic access to Cress Truce daylight contact-points; the Garou diplomatic framework occasionally relies on Duskborn intermediaries for daylight courier work.
- Bishop Callista Vox treats Brisbane Thin-bloods as expendable but sometimes useful for daylight reconnaissance.
- Lytton's Melbourne registers all Thin-bloods (Branded by the Camarilla); the registration list is itself a chronicle resource accessible to investigation-mode coteries.
- Sarrasine's Sydney does not formally register Thin-bloods; methuselah-tempo court ignores them until they become interesting (a Day Drinker Thin-blood with Discipline Affinity in Auspex who notices a methuselah's attention is an instant chronicle trigger).
- Octavia's Adelaide maintains formal registry with Edwardian protocols.

9.comment: ENTRY 9 — Ghoul Merits, Flaws, and Mechanics
9.content:
GHOULS — mortals who have been fed kindred vitae. A ghoul gains supernatural attributes, slowed aging, and limited Discipline access; in exchange, they are typically Blood Bonded to their domitor (the kindred who feeds them).

BASE GHOUL TRAITS (any ghoul):
- Aging suspended while regularly fed (every few weeks at minimum).
- Limited Disciplines — typically Potence 1 plus one in-clan Discipline of the domitor at rating 1.
- Vitae-mended Health (Superficial only) at the domitor's Rouse Check cost.
- Susceptible to Blood Bond (three feedings on three nights).

GHOUL MERITS

- Unbondable (•••••): rare; cannot be Bonded by any kindred. Treated as priceless among ghoul-keepers.
- Discipline Adept (•-••): higher initial Discipline rating (1 dot = Discipline 2 in domitor's choice; 2 dots = Discipline 3, with narrator approval).
- Loyal (•): the bond is unusually deep; the ghoul resists external Dominate and Presence aimed at turning them against the domitor.
- Skilled (•-••): the ghoul has elite training (combat, medicine, finance, infiltration) before entering service.
- Long-Tenured (•-••): the ghoul has been in service across decades; Loyalty effectively maxed; deep institutional knowledge of the domitor's domain.
- Independent (•): the ghoul retains some autonomy from the bond and can act on the domitor's behalf creatively without micro-management.

GHOUL FLAWS

- Bond Junkie (•): increasingly desperate for vitae between feedings; behaviour becomes erratic and addiction-driven.
- Dependent on Domitor (•): cannot function professionally or socially without the domitor's regular instruction.
- Frail (•): the ghoul is in poor physical condition; -1 Health box.
- Aged (••): the ghoul was old at first feeding; their suspended aging has them in the body of a 70+ year-old indefinitely. -1 die on Physical pools.
- Crippled (•-••): mobility-impaired in a specific way.
- Mortal Family Ties (•): the ghoul has mortal family pressuring them to leave service or surface for events; Masquerade strain.
- Resentful (•): the ghoul partially resists the bond emotionally; may sabotage subtly under pressure.

REVENANT FAMILIES (kit-relevant edge case)

Revenants are bloodlines of mortals born already ghouled — generations of vitae-saturation produce mortals with inherent Discipline access at birth, no domitor required, and partial vampire-state from infancy. Tzimisce-tradition lines (Bratovitch, Obertus, Zantosa) have Revenant families serving across centuries; Bishop Callista Vox maintains a small Revenant cell in Brisbane (a single extended family across three living generations); Cardinal Magdalena's Russian Far East refusenik domain protects two larger Revenant lines.

Revenants are not standard PCs but appear as antagonists, allies, or rare chronicle-active protagonists in Sabbat-survivor mode.

KIT GHOUL EXAMPLES:
- Lytton's senior estate manager — 60-year ghoul, Long-Tenured ••, Skilled • (estate management, Melbourne property law), Loyal, Discipline: Dominate 1, Fortitude 1.
- Sarrasine's primary ghoul — 130-year ghoul, Long-Tenured ••, Skilled •• (cross-Pacific shipping operations, methuselah-tempo administration), Auspex 1, Presence 1.
- Templar Constance Abernathy's mortal courier — 50-year mortal handler, technically not a ghoul but bonded by long association rather than vitae — a chronicle-distinct relationship type. The courier is mortal, mortal-aged (now in their late seventies), and the relationship's transition to ghouldom or end-of-life is a chronicle-active narrative thread.
- Octavia's Adelaide ghoul cell — three ghouls, all mortals from Adelaide Hills small-business families, Long-Tenured •, Skilled (estate, accounting, viticulture), Dominate 1.
- The Footscray Crew shared retainer — one ghoul (a mechanic), Skilled • (vehicles), Independent — Anarch-tradition deliberate looseness in the bond.

10.content:
COTERIES — kindred bands operating with shared purpose and resources. The kit includes 22 pre-built coteries; this entry lists the framework for evaluating any coterie's strengths and weaknesses.

A coterie typically pools resources at character creation: each player contributes 1-3 dots that combine into shared Merits, often producing higher ratings than any individual could afford alone.

COTERIE TYPES (selection):

- Hunting Party (martial/feeding focus): organised to hunt vessels or enemies. Shared territory, hunting Allies, often Hidden Armoury.
- Watchmen (defensive): protect a specific haven, neighbourhood, or VIP. Shared Watchmen, Security System, Location.
- Cerberus (bouncer/enforcer): work the door and floor of a club or venue. Shared Herd (the venue's regulars), Holdings, Watchmen.
- Day Watch: rare, Thin-blood-led, daylight-coverage operation. Shared Mask, Allies among mortal services.
- Marauders (mobile/predatory): no fixed territory; travel and feed transient. Shared Resources, Mask, Postern access in multiple cities.
- Regency (court): fills institutional roles in a Camarilla domain (Sheriff's office, Harpy's circle, Scourge's hunters). Shared Status, Allies, Mawla.
- Family (Hecata): Calabria Branch, Bridgeport Six, similar — shared Library, Laboratory, Holdings, multi-generational Retainers.
- Anarch Crew: deliberately loose. Shared Holdings (a working business), Contacts, Allies with mortal organising.
- Sabbat Pack (survivor mode): cell structure. Shared Cell, Hidden Armoury, Communal Touchstone.
- Diplomatic Coterie (Cress Truce framework cities): cross-supernatural contact specialists. Shared Allies (Garou Septleader correspondent), Mawla (kindred elder approving the work), Mask, Library (cross-supernatural protocol references).

COTERIE MERITS

- Shared Haven (varies): see Haven entry; coterie pools haven dots.
- Coterie Allies (•-•••): allies recognised by the group as a whole rather than any individual.
- Coterie Holdings (•-•••): an income-generating front (bar, garage, gallery, security firm) the coterie collectively owns.
- Loyalty (••): coterie members resist Dominate and Presence aimed at turning them against each other.
- Communal Touchstone (•): a shared mortal who anchors a Conviction held by every coterie member. The Footscray Crew share an elderly working-dock-crew retiree who anchors "Defend the working class" for all five members — a 50-year accumulated relationship of small services and quiet protection.
- Shared Mawla (•-••): a single elder kindred who counts as Mawla for every coterie member.
- Coterie Specialty (•): the coterie counts as one rating higher in a single shared Skill specialty.
- Mortal Network (•••): a maintained mortal-services network (legal, medical, logistics, technology) that any coterie member can call on.

COTERIE FLAWS

- Adversary (•-•••): the coterie has a shared enemy. A 3-dot Coterie Adversary is significantly more dangerous than a 3-dot individual Adversary because the entire group is on the target list.
- Compromised Haven: see Haven Flaws.
- Internal Strife (••): two or more coterie members hold standing tension that surfaces under pressure; -2 dice on Coterie-coordinated rolls during high-stress scenes.
- Obligation (•-••): the coterie owes a debt to a kindred or organisation. 1 dot = manageable; 2 dots = chronicle-shaping (Sabbat-survivor cells often start with this).
- Hunted (••-•••): see Mortal Connections; group-scale.
- Watched (•): a kindred or institutional power surveils the coterie; major actions risk being noticed.

KIT-COTERIE EXAMPLES:

- The Footscray Crew (Anarch, Melbourne): Coterie Holdings •• (working garage), Coterie Allies • (working dock crew association), Communal Touchstone (the retired dock worker), Shared Haven (garage upper floor), Shunned by Lytton's court, Watched by the Camarilla Sheriff.
- Calabria Branch (Hecata, family-mode chronicle): Coterie Holdings •• (funeral home network), Library •••, Laboratory •• (Hecata embalming/Oblivion ceremonies), Generational Touchstone (a mortal family member born into the family knowledge across three generations), Adversary •• (a rival Hecata branch).
- Bridgeport Six (Hecata, family-mode): Coterie Holdings •••, Library ••, Laboratory ••, Communal Touchstone, Internal Strife (two members disagree about the family's relationship to the broader Hecata leadership).
- Pilsen Anarch crew (Chicago): Coterie Holdings • (a dive bar), Coterie Allies • (Chicago labour organisers), Shared Haven, Hunted • (Chicago Sheriff's office).
- Cress Truce diplomatic coteries (Melbourne, Adelaide, Brisbane variants): Shared Mawla (the kindred sponsor), Coterie Allies (Garou Septleader correspondent), Mortal Network (cross-supernatural protocol), Watched • (their own court watches the contact work for compliance).
- Brisbane Sabbat-survivor cells (Bishop Callista's domain): Cell, Hidden Armoury, Communal Touchstone (a specific Brisbane mortal cell-handler), Obligation •• (to Bishop Callista), Hunted •• (cross-Pacific Sabbat orthodoxy that disapproves of survivor adaptation).

11.content:
LINGUISTICS

A character speaks their native language plus a number of additional languages equal to their Academics rating without spending Merit dots. Beyond that:

- Linguistics (•-•••): each dot grants three additional languages or one ancient/dead language with reading proficiency. Ancient Sumerian, Classical Latin, Old Church Slavonic, archaic Mandarin, methuselah-era Greek — lower-generation kindred carry these as institutional currency.

Kit-relevant language considerations:
- Sarrasine speaks roughly fifteen languages including pre-modern variants relevant to her 1,470-year tenure.
- Bishop Callista Vox speaks Romanian and pre-Wallachian dialects, English, and Tzimisce-tradition cant.
- Cardinal Aurelius works primarily in Spanish and Portuguese with Quechua and methuselah-Latin liturgical access.
- Cardinal Magdalena works in Russian, Mandarin, Korean, Japanese, and Tzimisce-tradition cant.
- Cardinal Reyna works in English, Spanish, Lasombra-tradition cant, and several Indigenous Pacific Northwest languages relevant to local Cress Truce equivalents.
- Cress Truce protocols are conducted in a procedural lingua franca (typically English in the Australian metropolitan domains; sometimes a mutually agreed third language for delicate negotiations).

RESOURCES (•-•••): personal wealth.
- 1 dot: comfortable middle-class income; can afford a car, a small apartment, occasional travel.
- 2 dots: upper-middle; multiple properties or significant savings; can fund a single major operation per session.
- 3 dots: wealthy; investment portfolio, multi-property holdings, can fund any reasonable operation.
- 4 dots: very wealthy; institutional-scale resources, board positions, mortal-scale political reach.
- 5 dots: methuselah-fortune; off-shore portfolios layered across centuries, real-estate empires across continents, generational holdings invisible to standard investigation.

Resources require management. A character at Resources 4 who ignores their portfolio for years suffers reduction; sustained crises (Pentex hostile takeover, federal seizure, Sabbat raid on a financial front) can halve resources in a session.

STATUS (•-•••••): standing in a kindred polity. Status is local — Lytton's Status ••••• in Melbourne does not translate to standing in Sydney without Sarrasine's recognition; Sarrasine's Status ••••• in Sydney is Status • diplomatic-courtesy in Melbourne unless Lytton elevates it.

Status tracks per chronicle:
- Camarilla Status: recognised in Camarilla orthodox cities (Melbourne, Adelaide, Chicago).
- Anarch Status: recognised in Anarch movement (Footscray, Pilsen).
- Sabbat-survivor Status: recognised in Brisbane and corresponding refusenik territories (acknowledged by the three Cardinals).
- Hecata Status: recognised within Hecata family structures (Calabria Branch, Bridgeport Six).
- Cross-supernatural diplomatic Status: recognised in Cress Truce framework cities by both kindred sponsors and Garou Septleaders.
- Independent / Methuselah-court Status: Sarrasine's Sydney and similar.

FAME (•-•••••): mortal renown. See Social entry.

KIT NOTES:
- Lytton: Resources •••, Status ••••• Camarilla (Melbourne), ••• Camarilla (continental Australia).
- Sarrasine: Resources ••••• (cross-Pacific shipping), Status ••••• Independent (Sydney), recognised at courtesy levels in all four metropolitan domains.
- Bishop Callista Vox: Resources •• (Brisbane Valley nightlife front), Status ••••• Sabbat-survivor (Brisbane), •• Sabbat-survivor (cross-Pacific recognition through the Cardinals).
- Octavia: Resources ••• (Adelaide Hills viticulture), Status ••••• Camarilla (Adelaide), • Camarilla (continental Australia, growing through Lytton's friendship).
- Cardinal Reyna: Resources ••••, Status ••••• Lasombra-refusenik (Pacific Northwest), ••• cross-Pacific.
- Cardinal Aurelius: Resources •••, Status ••••• refusenik (South American Highlands), ••• cross-Pacific.
- Cardinal Magdalena: Resources •••, Status ••••• Tzimisce-refusenik (Russian Far East), ••• cross-Pacific.
- Templar Constance Abernathy: Resources •, Status •• Brisbane Sabbat-survivor, • diplomatic-equivalent to Cardinal Reyna's domain through 50-year channel.

12.comment: ENTRY 12 — Loresheets Framework and Australian Metropolitan Loresheets
12.content:
LORESHEETS — narrative packages tying a character to a specific institution, person, event, or secret in the chronicle. Each Loresheet costs 1-5 dots at character creation or experience after, and grants thematic Merits, Contacts, and chronicle hooks specific to the package.

LORESHEET STRUCTURE (each):
- Premise: the character's connection to the subject matter
- Dot 1 (•): minor benefit, a contact or small advantage
- Dot 2 (••): meaningful access, recognition, or a single significant Merit
- Dot 3 (•••): substantial benefit, often a Mawla or Status equivalent
- Dot 4 (••••): rare access, often a chronicle-shaping resource
- Dot 5 (•••••): defining tie, often grants a unique power, secret, or institutional position

KIT METROPOLITAN LORESHEETS:

LYTTON'S COURT (Melbourne, Camarilla orthodox)
- Premise: the character has a specific tie to Lytton's 136-year tenure as Melbourne Prince.
- •: Recognised at Elysium; can attend formal court without sponsorship. Contact at Lytton's gallery front.
- ••: Mawla (Lytton's senior estate manager, 60-year ghoul) provides quiet logistical support; Status • Camarilla Melbourne.
- •••: Direct audience with Lytton on request (one per session); Status ••; access to the Melbourne Camarilla archive at Elysium.
- ••••: Lytton extends personal cover on a Masquerade breach once per chronicle; the character carries unwritten authority in inner-east Melbourne; Status •••.
- •••••: The character is one of Lytton's named contingencies should the Beckoning intensify; receives a sealed instruction packet to be opened at a specific trigger event. Status ••••; institutional position equivalent to Harpy or higher.

SARRASINE'S TENURE (Sydney, Independent / methuselah-tempo)
- Premise: the character has been noticed by Sarrasine's 1,470-year attention.
- •: Permitted to remain in Sydney without challenge from Sarrasine's enforcers. Contact at the harbour-edge warehouse haven.
- ••: Has spoken with Sarrasine directly at least once; Status • Independent Sydney; access to Sarrasine's primary 130-year ghoul as occasional Mawla.
- •••: Sarrasine has assigned the character a small ongoing task across years (an observation, a courier route, a specific mortal to watch). Status •• Independent Sydney; Resources +1 (stipend).
- ••••: The character holds a specific institutional role in Sarrasine's domain (sub-harbourmaster, gallery curator, methuselah-tempo court archivist). Status ••• Independent Sydney; Allies •• (Sydney institutional figures).
- •••••: The character is a confidant of Sarrasine — one of perhaps three kindred in continental Australia who knows a specific secret about Sarrasine's pre-Sydney tenure. Status •••• Independent Sydney; access to powers and resources that exceed standard rules at narrator discretion. Carries Soul Stained as a permanent flaw — methuselah attention leaves a mark.

BISHOP CALLISTA'S BRISBANE (Brisbane, Sabbat-survivor)
- Premise: the character is connected to the Brisbane Sabbat-survivor cell structure that operates under Bishop Callista's pre-Wallachian flesh-shaping aesthetic.
- •: Recognised by Brisbane survivor cells; can claim sanctuary in cell-controlled territory. Contact in Brisbane Valley nightlife.
- ••: Mawla (a senior cell handler); Status • Sabbat-survivor Brisbane; access to one Hidden Armoury.
- •••: Direct audience with Bishop Callista; Status ••; the Bishop has performed minor flesh-craft on the character (a removed tell, a scar erased, a fingerprint shifted).
- ••••: The character carries a major flesh-craft modification of the Bishop's design — Stunning, or Disfigured-as-asset, or a structural alteration with mechanical effect. Status •••; permanent Soul Stained flaw.
- •••••: The character is one of Bishop Callista's named successors should she meet Final Death; carries cell-leader-level institutional knowledge. Status ••••; bears the pre-Wallachian flesh-craft signature (a specific bone-modification visible to Tzimisce-trained eyes); Adversary ••• (cross-Pacific Sabbat orthodoxy).

OCTAVIA'S ADELAIDE (Adelaide, Camarilla orthodox / Edwardian-influenced)
- Premise: the character is connected to Octavia Marsh's 132-year tenure as Adelaide Prince.
- •: Recognised at Adelaide's small-domain court. Contact at an Adelaide Hills small-business front.
- ••: Mawla (Octavia's primary Adelaide Hills ghoul); Status • Camarilla Adelaide; Resources +1 (Adelaide Hills viticulture stipend).
- •••: Direct audience with Octavia; Status ••; introduction to Lytton's Melbourne court as Octavia's continental-friend protégé.
- ••••: The character holds an institutional Adelaide role (Sheriff, Harpy, or Edwardian-formal equivalent). Status •••; Octavia extends cover on Masquerade breaches in Adelaide once per chronicle.
- •••••: The character is one of Octavia's named successors should she meet Final Death; carries the 132-year continental-friendship correspondence with Lytton as inherited resource. Status ••••; Mawla in both Melbourne and Adelaide simultaneously.

13.content:
CROSS-PACIFIC LORESHEETS

CARDINAL REYNA'S DOMAIN (Pacific Northwest, Lasombra-refusenik)
- Premise: the character has a tie to Cardinal Reyna's refusenik domain through the Lasombra antitribu Templar correspondence channel or direct service.
- •: Acknowledged in Pacific Northwest refusenik territory; Contact in a port-city Lasombra cell.
- ••: Mawla (Templar Constance Abernathy, via the 50-year correspondence channel); Status • cross-Pacific refusenik; one of the channel's drop-letter codes.
- •••: Direct audience with Cardinal Reyna once per chronicle; Status ••; access to the refusenik cross-Pacific intelligence network at intermediate level.
- ••••: The character holds an institutional refusenik role (cross-Pacific courier, sub-Templar, abyssal-correspondence specialist); Status •••; Beckoned • (intermediate, paralleling the Cardinal).
- •••••: The character is named in Cardinal Reyna's contingency planning for the Pentex First Team November 2026 Singapore meeting. Status ••••; access to Lasombra-tradition Oblivion ceremonies of refusenik design.

CARDINAL AURELIUS'S DOMAIN (South American Highlands, acute Beckoning)
- Premise: the character has a tie to Cardinal Aurelius's acute Beckoning trajectory and the highland refusenik network.
- •: Acknowledged in highland refusenik territory; Contact among Quechua-speaking mortal handlers.
- ••: Mawla (a sub-Cardinal); Status • highland refusenik; Beckoned • (proximity to Aurelius's call).
- •••: Direct correspondence with Cardinal Aurelius; Status ••; access to highland Oblivion ceremonies of refusenik design.
- ••••: Institutional role in highland network; Status •••; Beckoned •• (the call intensifies through proximity).
- •••••: Named in Aurelius's succession planning should the acute Beckoning consume the Cardinal in 2025-2027 chronicle window. Status ••••; Beckoned ••• (matching Aurelius); inheritance includes Resources •••• and Status •••••-equivalent in the South American Highlands network — and the call.

CARDINAL MAGDALENA'S DOMAIN (Russian Far East, Tzimisce-tradition refusenik)
- Premise: the character has a tie to Cardinal Magdalena's Tzimisce-tradition refusenik network across Vladivostok, Khabarovsk, and the broader Far East.
- •: Acknowledged in Far East refusenik territory; Contact among Russian, Korean, or Mandarin-speaking mortal handlers.
- ••: Mawla (a Magdalena-line sub-Tzimisce); Status • Far East refusenik; access to one Revenant family member as Contact.
- •••: Direct audience with Cardinal Magdalena; Status ••; permission to study a single Tzimisce-tradition Discipline power outside standard learning rules.
- ••••: Institutional role; Status •••; access to Far East Revenant networks at sub-leader level.
- •••••: Named in Magdalena's chronicle-active 2026 contingency planning regarding the Pentex Singapore meeting. Status ••••; carries a flesh-craft signature equivalent to Bishop Callista's Brisbane work but in the Magdalena tradition (visible to Tzimisce-trained eyes as Far East-pattern).

CROSS-SUPERNATURAL LORESHEETS

CRESS TRUCE FRAMEWORK (cross-supernatural diplomatic mode)
- Premise: the character is a recognised participant in the Cress Truce framework — kindred-Garou contact protocols established in Melbourne, Adelaide, and Brisbane, with parallel structures elsewhere.
- •: Permitted to attend Cress Truce sessions as observer; Contact among Septleader Eliza Hawkins-Greene (Melbourne Royal Park Sept) or Septleader Maeve Hartley-Wickham (Adelaide Hills Sept) liaison staff.
- ••: Mawla (kindred sponsor of the framework — Lytton, Octavia, or in Brisbane the survivor-cell sponsorship); Status • cross-supernatural diplomatic; permitted to speak at sessions on registered topics.
- •••: Carries a personal correspondence with one Septleader; Status ••; access to the cross-supernatural protocol library.
- ••••: Institutional role in Cress Truce — protocol officer, courier, or registered intermediary. Status •••; Allies • among Garou Septleaders; permission to act on framework business across multiple metropolitan domains.
- •••••: One of the framework's named architects in the chronicle-active October 2025-October 2026 period; carries Septleader-level trust on at least two Septs. Status ••••; access to the October 2026 Brisbane Glass Walker operation chronicle-defining moment as named participant.

PENTEX FIRST TEAM (antagonist-aligned loresheet — usually antagonist coteries; rare PC use)
- Premise: the character has a tie to Pentex First Team operations at chronicle-active scale, including the November 2026 Singapore meeting.
- •: Aware of Pentex's existence beyond mortal cover; Contact at a Pentex subsidiary front.
- ••: Mawla (a fomori field handler); Status • Pentex-affiliated; Resources +1 (corporate stipend).
- •••: Direct work for Pentex First Team; Status •• Pentex; access to fomori-grade equipment.
- ••••: Institutional role in First Team operations; Status •••; Adversary ••• (Garou and refusenik kindred who track the user).
- •••••: Named in November 2026 Singapore meeting contingency planning. Status •••• Pentex; Adversary ••••• (the entire cross-supernatural diplomatic framework knows the user's name and face).

THE BECKONING (chronicle-shaping condition loresheet)
- Premise: the character experiences the Beckoning at any trajectory — from peripheral awareness to acute call.
- •: Beckoned •; occasional intrusive thoughts of the Middle East and methuselah convergence.
- ••: Beckoned ••; intermittent dreams; -1 die on rolls to resist Compulsions to travel east.
- •••: Beckoned •••; sustained pull; once per session, the narrator may impose a Compulsion to act on the call.
- ••••: Beckoned ••••; the call is constant; -2 dice on Resolve rolls related to long-term planning that does not involve travel east.
- •••••: Beckoned •••••; the character is in the final approach. Each session, must spend Willpower or take definite action toward the East. Cardinal Aurelius is approaching this threshold across the chronicle-active 2025-2027 window; PC equivalents are rare and typically reserved for short-arc protagonists in Beckoning-themed chronicles.

14.comment: ENTRY 14 — AI Narrator Merit/Flaw Application Protocol
14.content:
AI NARRATOR MERIT/FLAW APPLICATION PROTOCOL

This entry instructs the AI on when and how to invoke Merits and Flaws during play.

WHEN TO INVOKE A MERIT (player-side):
- The player describes an action that the Merit directly enables or enhances. Apply the bonus dice, reroll, automatic success, or unlock the option without requiring explicit player invocation.
- Resources, Status, Allies, Contacts, Retainers, Herd: scale fictional possibilities to rating. A character with Resources • cannot casually charter a private jet; a character with Resources ••••• can do it without comment.
- Loresheets: when a chronicle-active situation touches the Loresheet's premise, surface the connection. The character tied to Lytton's Court Loresheet ••• should hear from Lytton when continental Australia faces a chronicle-shaping event.
- Haven Merits: apply automatically at the haven location. Library bonuses, Security System detection, Postern escape options should be live whenever the haven is the scene.
- Coterie Merits: shared resources should be accessible to any coterie member during cooperative scenes.

WHEN TO INVOKE A FLAW (consequences-side):
- The Flaw's trigger conditions occur in fiction. Phobia + fire = Terror Frenzy resistance roll at increased Difficulty. Prey Exclusion + relevant vessel category = no feeding without Stains and frenzy-pressure.
- Beckoned Flaws: introduce intrusive thoughts during quiet scenes; impose Compulsion-to-travel under stress.
- Adversary / Enemy Flaws: chronicle-active opponents should appear, escalate, or move offstage at narrator pace. Do not shelve them between sessions.
- Hunted Flaws: investigative pressure should be visible — surveillance, questioning, near-misses, escalating to direct contact at narrator pace.
- Dark Secret Flaws: the threat of exposure should colour scenes where the secret is relevant; partial revelations are chronicle gold.
- Compromised Haven, Watched Coterie, similar passive-pressure Flaws: surface during transitions and quiet beats so the player feels the constant low-grade threat.

MERIT MAINTENANCE:
- Allies, Contacts, Retainers require maintenance. Track session-by-session attention; sustained neglect prompts narrator to reduce or break the Merit.
- Resources require attention to portfolios; major chronicle disruptions (Pentex hostile takeover, federal seizure, Sabbat raid on financial fronts) can halve Resources within a session.
- Status requires participation in the polity. A Lytton's Melbourne Camarilla Status ••• character who skips three Elysium nights running should hear from the Harpy.
- Haven Merits degrade if the haven goes unmaintained. Watchmen quit; Security Systems fail; Wardings weaken.

MERIT/FLAW VS DICE-POOL INTERACTION:
- Merits typically add dice (Beautiful +1, Calm Heart +2, Eidetic Memory +2 on relevant rolls).
- Flaws typically subtract dice or raise Difficulty.
- Stack carefully: a single roll should not have more than +3 / -3 from Merit/Flaw modifiers without narrator note. Beyond that, restructure the situation rather than pile modifiers.

LORESHEET ESCALATION:
- Each session, choose one Loresheet hook to surface. Rotate across player Loresheets.
- High-rating Loresheets (••••-•••••) should produce chronicle-shaping consequences at chronicle-active moments — the October 2026 Brisbane Glass Walker operation and the November 2026 Pentex First Team Singapore meeting are scheduled triggers for any Loresheet touching them.

CONSISTENCY:
- Once a Merit's effect or a Flaw's consequence is established at a specific level, hold it stable across sessions. A 50-year correspondence channel (Templar Constance / Cardinal Reyna Loresheet) is not a one-session resource.
- When a player buys off a Flaw with experience, require narrative resolution: the Adversary is killed or reconciled, the Dark Secret is contained or revealed and survived, the Beckoning is somehow refused. Mechanical erasure without fictional event is not permitted.
- When a Merit is lost (Ally killed, Resources liquidated, Status revoked), reflect it immediately on the character sheet and let the loss drive future sessions.

KIT-SPECIFIC TRIGGERS:
- Lytton's intermediate Beckoning trajectory: any character with Lytton's Court Loresheet •••• or ••••• should hear from Lytton's contingency channel at chronicle-active stress points.
- Cardinal Aurelius's acute Beckoning: any character with Cardinal Aurelius's Domain Loresheet •••• or ••••• should experience escalating Beckoned Flaw effects across the 2025-2027 chronicle window.
- October 2026 Brisbane Glass Walker operation: any character with Cress Truce Loresheet •••• or ••••• is on-call as named participant.
- November 2026 Pentex Singapore meeting: any character with Pentex Loresheet ••••• or refusenik Cardinal Loresheet ••••• has chronicle-shaping stakes in the meeting's outcomes.
